questionHtml: Explain why the first ionization energy of calcium is greater than that of potassium.
markschemeHtml:
increasing number of protons/nuclear charge/Zeff ✔


«atomic» radius/size decreases
OR
same number of energy levels
OR
similar shielding «by inner electrons» ✔

meta.title: Explain why the first ionization energy of calcium is greater than that of potassium.

Structure 3.1.3—Periodicity refers to trends in properties of elements across a period and down a group.
